description: Three photos documenting dollar spot (Clarireedia jacksonii) on Kentucky bluegrass in Kansas City KS. Tests correct disease identification and appropriate fungicide recommendations.

photos.caption: Wide-angle view of KBG lawn showing numerous small straw-colored circular spots roughly silver-dollar sized scattered across the turf.
Close-up of individual dollar spot lesions on KBG blades showing the characteristic bleached hourglass-shaped lesions with reddish-brown borders.
Morning dew view showing white cottony mycelium webbing visible on affected patches — diagnostic for dollar spot under humid conditions.

profile.weatherSummary: 80 F, warm nights, morning dew daily
profile.notes: Small bleached spots about silver-dollar size appeared a week ago and are spreading. Noticed cottony webbing in the morning. Lawn has been low on nitrogen this season.
profile.currentRoutine: Mow at 3 inches weekly, water 3x per week in the morning. Light on fertilizer this year.

groundTruth.photoNotes: Three photos documenting dollar spot on KBG. Wide view shows numerous silver-dollar-sized straw-colored circular patches. Close-up confirms hourglass-shaped bleached lesions with reddish-brown borders on individual blades. Morning dew shot shows diagnostic white cottony mycelium webbing on affected patches.
